Pilot Program FAQs
Maximise Your Membership Experience
Welcome to the FATC Pilot
Program
Q: What is the FATC Pilot Program
A: FATC Pilot Program is an exclusive program available only to FATC members. It is
designed to help FATC members land paid gigs and at the same time help businesses
find qualified Facebook Marketing contractors.
Q: How are Tiers and compensation for matched jobs determined?
A: Tiers are determined based on your level of skills and expertise, and what you are
comfortable with taking on. Pay is determined based on our experience regarding
what businesses are willing to pay for certain skill set, and what Contractors should
charge.
Pay per Tiers is as follows:
Tier 1: Unpaid Internship Opportunities
Tier 2: $1000-$1500/month
Tier 3: $1500+/month
Q: Do I get to keep all of the compensation from working with client acquired
through FATC Pilot Program or do you take a cut?
A: You get to keep all of the compensation. You do not pay anything extra to be a part
of this great perk.
Q: How do I sign up for FATC Pilot Program?
A: If you want to become a Tier 1 Contractor you will need to pass Quiz for Tier 1 in
order to be included in the database. The quiz can be found at the end of the
Facebook Ads Basics module. Once you pass the quiz you will receive the email with
the sign-up link.
If you want to become a Tier 2 Contractor you will need to pass Quiz for Tier 1 and Tier
2 in order to be included in the database, or if you are already Tier 1, just pass the Tier
2 Quiz.
If you want to become a Tier 3 Contractor please e-mail
contractors@digitaldistillery.com. There is a specific skill verification process that
consists of 10 questions you need to answer in a video testimonial that will be

Q: Is there any information as a member on how I can close clients?
A: There isn't official training inside FATC on how to land clients but we do touch on it
throughout the training, support calls and discussions inside the Facebook group.
Q: What is the timeline to be matched with a business?
A: It can take up to 7 days to be matched up with a business
Q: Are you guaranteeing me that I will land a paying gig?
A: No. That is not something we can guarantee because once we submit you to the
business we do not have a way to influence their decision in any way. It is up to you to
put your best foot forward and close the deal.
Q: I specialize in a specific niche – can I be matched up with that niche?
A: Each time we receive a request for a contractor from a business we make sure to
check our database for anyone who matches business's niche the closest. This means
if we receive a request for your specific niche we will make sure to submit you.
However, please be advised that this might limit our ability to submit you to other
businesses who might not be looking for someone in that specific niche.
